title: Opportunity Cost — Every Dollar Has a Job
learningObjectives: Understand opportunity cost as the road not taken
See how every dollar spent is a choice between two futures
Apply the 'best alternative use' test before spending
keyPrinciples: Every dollar you spend is a dollar that can't be invested
Opportunity cost = the best thing you gave up
Rich people assign every dollar a job before spending
storyPrompts: {name} buys a {item} for {amount}. Nice. But their mentor pulls out a calculator: 'That {amount}, invested at 10% annually, becomes $1,400 in 20 years. You didn't just spend {amount}. You spent $1,400.' The number hangs in the air.
Two paths diverge at the checkout counter. Path A: buy the {item} now. Path B: invest the money and buy something 10x better in 5 years. {name} knows both paths exist every single time they spend.
Every dollar in {name}'s wallet has a tiny sign on it: 'Assign me a job.' Spend means today. Save means safety. Invest means tomorrow. The dollars don't care — but {name} has to choose.

decisionCards.scenario: You're about to spend {amount} on something fun. Show the opportunity cost.
decisionCards.context: What does $50 invested today become in 20 years at 10%?

choices.label: Spend it, live now
choices.opportunityCost: $50 at 10% for 20 years = $336. That's the real price tag.

choices.label: Invest half, spend half
choices.description: Honor both present and future you.

choices.label: Invest all of it
choices.opportunityCost: The full $336 future value. Every dollar worked.

decisionCards.scenario: You got a $200 raise in your allowance this month. What's its job?
decisionCards.context: More money = more responsibility for where it goes.

choices.label: Keep lifestyle the same, save the raise
choices.description: Lifestyle inflation is the wealth killer.

choices.label: Invest the raise, keep lifestyle modest
choices.description: The owner's response to more income.

decisionCards.scenario: You realize you have {amount} of unused subscriptions per month.
decisionCards.context: Subscriptions are sneaky opportunity cost machines.

choices.label: Keep them — I might use them someday
Keep only what you've used in the past month

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You've tracked your spending this month. Every dollar is visible. What's the verdict?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Awareness is the first step. Action is the second.

choices.label: Some wasted dollars, but overall fine
choices.description: Awareness without change is just guilt.

choices.label: Build a zero-based budget going forward

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You find $100 in an old coat. What does opportunity cost say?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Found money reveals your default financial instincts.

choices.label: Treat it as a fun windfall
choices.description: It was never in the budget anyway!

choices.label: Invest it all — best use of surprise money
choices.description: You were fine without it. Let it grow.

choices.label: Use it to start the business idea you've had

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Your entire financial month visualized. One thing to change next month?
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Reflection is the final skill of the Owner.

forecasts: You mastered opportunity cost this week! Investment events become higher-yield next week.
Several opportunity costs were missed. Expect stronger 'spend now' temptations next week.
Your Awareness trait is maxing out! Hidden discount opportunities appear next week.
Consistent bucket discipline unlocks a bonus session event next week.
